description.text: Click the word "Contact" to cycle through placeholder texts.
Visit the <404page> to see another animation.
If you like Cyberpunk 2077 and own a Garmin, checkout my <watch_face>.

eggs.egg: The whole theme of this site is cyberpunk.
You can see the Cyberpunk 2077 menu cursor by hovering my personal slide photos. There's also an Arasaka micro-graphic.
There's a Johnny Silverhand quote in the contact form placeholder text.
The space ship is loosely modeled off the Planet Express ship.
The contact form placeholder text has the Professor's tagline.
The wind, water, earth, and fire logo can be seen as a micro-graphic on the personal photo.
The contact form is on a Multipass.
The contact form placeholder text has a Leeloo Dallas line.
The 404 page's drifting particle field is a nod to the Star Gate sequence in 2001: A Space Odyssey.
The splash slide planet is the color of Arrakis on the Dune book cover.
The contact form placeholder text has Paul's mantra.
Clicking my name in the splash slide makes the ship fire an arcade game style bullet.
There's a green portal floating on the splash slide.
The contact form placeholder text has a Rick tagline.
The splash slide has two buttons, one red pill and one blue pill.
The contact form placeholder text has a line from Morpheus.
The progress bars are based on UI seen at Nerv HQ.
The blueprint background in the Programs section has a diagram of the angels.
The double wave animation behind the contact form is inspired by EVA sync rate UI.
The send button in the contact form was designed to resemble the focus in Horizon Zero Dawn and Forbidden West.
This is a stretch but the contact form is a reference to Contact in name.
There is a quote from Ellie Arroway in the placeholder text.
The Personal slide's layout is based on the scene where Mr. Incredible discovers the Omnidroid project.
In the contact form prompt I promise to phone back.
The contact form placeholder text has a line from a replicant.
The contact form placeholder text has a rebel secret code.
The contact form placeholder text has a line Tony Stark says.
The contact form placeholder text has a line Rocky says a lot.
An asterisk Kurt Vonnegut once drew can be seen as a micro-graphic in my photos. I'm a big fan of Player Piano and Slaughterhouse 5.
The Weyland-Yutani logo can be found as a micro-graphic on my personal slide.
A few of the micro-graphics on my photos are the alien text from Arrival.
The blurb on the 404 page is a play on the iconic "Houston..." line.
The cards on the Experience slide are old punch cards, the kind my mom used in school to program. Not really sci-fi because it's real.

description.text: Not really sci-fi because this is a real organization.

eggs.egg: The program blueprints feature the diagram on the golden Voyager Interstellar Record.
The splash slide is an animation based on a NASA JPL poster called 'The Grand Tour.'
